LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, AND OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. ON A DEMURRER, ALL FACTS PLED IN THE COMPLAINT ARE ASSUMED TO BE TRUE, REGARDLESS OF IMPROBABILITY. FOR THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS WITH THE REQUISITE PARTICULARITY TO ALLEGE PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE AGAINST DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA ("DEFENDANT"). FOR THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE ELDER OR DEPENDENT ABUSE. THE ALLEGATIONS OF RECKLESS, OPPRESSION, FRAUD AND MALICE ARE CONCLUSORY. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE THAT DEFENDANT'S NEGLECT OF THE DECEDENT WAS RECKLESS OR DONE WITH OPPRESSION, FRAUD OR MALICE. SINGLE ORDER SIGNED FOR BOTH MOTIONS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend as to the second cause of action, and overruled as to the first cause of action. On a demurrer, the facts pled in the complaint are assumed to be true. For the first cause of action, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts with the requisite particularity to allege professional negligence against Defendant City and County San Francisco ("Defendant"). For the second cause of action, Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege elder or dependent abuse. The allegations of reckless, oppression, fraud and malice are conclusory. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend the second cause of action, if he can do so in good faith, to allege that Defendant's neglect of the Decedent was reckless or done with oppression, fraud or malice. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. rULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Granted, without leave to amend. Plaintiff neither opposes the motion nor seeks any claim for treble damages. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-10-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained, with 10 days leave to amend as to the second cause of action, and overruled as to the first cause of action. On a demurrer, all facts pled in the complaint are assumed to be true, regardless of improbability. For the first cause of action, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts with the requisite particularity to allege professional negligence against Defendant Regents of the University of California ("Defendant"). For the second cause of action, Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege elder or dependent abuse. The allegations of reckless, oppression, fraud and malice are conclusory. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend the second cause of action, if he can do so in good faith, to allege that Defendant's neglect of the Decedent was reckless or done with oppression, fraud or malice. Single order signed for both motions. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185.
